summary: Any member of a course can edit or delete another member's post by supplying that post_id. The application verifies only course membership and never checks that the caller owns the target post or holds an explicitly authorized moderation role.

evidence_snippets.reason: Any logged-in course member can load the edit page for any post in the course. The route does not verify that the current user is the post author or an authorized moderator.
All post mutation paths require only course membership, then directly update or delete the user-supplied post_id without any ownership or moderation authorization check.
The write operations are scoped only by course_id and post_id, with no author_id or equivalent ownership constraint.

root_cause: Post mutation endpoints treat course membership as sufficient authorization and omit an ownership or moderator check for the specific post being edited or deleted.
repair_plan: Load the target post before rendering the edit page or applying any mutation, verify that the current user is the post author or holds an explicitly authorized course moderation role, and reject unauthorized requests. Where practical, scope repository updates and deletes by both post_id and author_id unless a separately verified staff path is used.
